are forested [wetlands](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland") in which the dominant trees are lowland [conifers](/wiki/Conifers "Conifers") such as northern white cedar (*[Thuja occidentalis](/wiki/Thuja_occidentalis "Thuja occidentalis")*). The soil in these swamp areas is typically saturated for most of the growing season and is occasionally inundated by seasonal storms or by winter snow melt.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Forested wetlands dominated by conifers

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Globe icon. | The examples and perspective in this article **deal primarily with North America and do not represent a [worldwide view](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Countering_systemic_bias "Wikipedia:WikiProject Countering systemic bias") of the subject**. You may [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit), discuss the issue on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Coniferous_swamp "Talk:Coniferous swamp"), or [create a new article](/wiki/Wikipedia:Article_wizard "Wikipedia:Article wizard"), as appropriate. *(April 2015)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c5/Dead_Stream_Swamp_MI.jpg/250px-Dead_Stream_Swamp_MI.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dead_Stream_Swamp_MI.jpg)

[Dead Stream Swamp](/wiki/Dead_Stream_Flooding_State_Wildlife_Management_Area "Dead Stream Flooding State Wildlife Management Area"), [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"): a northern white cedar swamp

**Coniferous swamps** are forested [wetlands](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland") in which the dominant trees are lowland [conifers](/wiki/Conifers "Conifers") such as northern white cedar (*[Thuja occidentalis](/wiki/Thuja_occidentalis "Thuja occidentalis")*). The soil in these swamp areas is typically saturated for most of the growing season and is occasionally inundated by seasonal storms or by winter snow melt.

The substrate is usually organic in nature and may contain [peat](/wiki/Peat "Peat") in varying amounts, or it may be composed entirely of [muck](/wiki/Hydric_soil "Hydric soil"). The swamp substrate is typically nutrient-rich and neutral to [alkaline](/wiki/Alkaline "Alkaline"), but can be [acidic](/wiki/Acidic "Acidic") and nutrient-poor.

Coniferous swamps vary in composition, with different species of conifer dominating, and varying amounts of deciduous hardwoods growing within the swamp. A wide diversity of plants is represented within the swamps, with certain species dominating in a variety of [microhabitats](/wiki/Microhabitat "Microhabitat") dependent on factors such as available sunlight (as in cases of trees downed by wind or disease), [Soil pH](/wiki/Soil_pH "Soil pH"), standing groundwater, and differences of elevation within the swamp such as [tussocks](/wiki/Tussock_(grass) "Tussock (grass)") and [nurse logs](/wiki/Nurse_log "Nurse log").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The different types of coniferous swamps are named according to their dominant trees. In [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"), a *rich conifer swamp* is dominated by northern white cedar and typically occurs south of the climatic [tension zone](/wiki/Tension_zone "Tension zone") throughout the [Midwest](/wiki/Midwest "Midwest") and northeastern United States and adjacent areas in [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"). North of this climatic tension zone, tamarack (*[Larix laricina](/wiki/Larix_laricina "Larix laricina")*) is the dominant species of conifer in [minerotrophic](/wiki/Minerotrophic "Minerotrophic") wetlands classified as *rich tamarack swamp*. A roughly equal mix of hardwood trees and conifers is known as a *hardwood-conifer swamp*.

## Rich conifer swamp

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Rich conifer swamp")]

### Flora

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Flora")]

#### Trees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Trees")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a6/Eastern_Arborvitae_%28Thuja_occidentalis%29_bark_and_foliage.jpg/250px-Eastern_Arborvitae_%28Thuja_occidentalis%29_bark_and_foliage.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eastern_Arborvitae_(Thuja_occidentalis)_bark_and_foliage.jpg)

[Bark](/wiki/Bark_(botany) "Bark (botany)") of the northern white cedar

A variety of both [evergreen](/wiki/Evergreen "Evergreen") and [deciduous](/wiki/Deciduous "Deciduous") trees may be present in the rich conifer swamp in addition to the dominant species.

* *[Thuja occidentalis](/wiki/Thuja_occidentalis "Thuja occidentalis")*: Northern white cedar, the dominant conifer, also known as arborvitae, a common landscape specimen in northern U.S. states and Canada.
* *[Abies balsamea](/wiki/Abies_balsamea "Abies balsamea")*: Balsam fir
* *[Acer rubrum](/wiki/Acer_rubrum "Acer rubrum")*: Red maple
* *[Betula papyrifera](/wiki/Betula_papyrifera "Betula papyrifera")*: Paper birch
* *[Chamaecyparis thyoides](/wiki/Chamaecyparis_thyoides "Chamaecyparis thyoides")*: Atlantic white-cedar
* *[Cornus stolonifera](/wiki/Cornus_stolonifera "Cornus stolonifera")*: Red-osier dogwood
* *[Cornus florida](/wiki/Cornus_florida "Cornus florida")*: Flowering dogwood
* *[Larix laricina](/wiki/Larix_laricina "Larix laricina")*: Tamarack
* *[Picea mariana](/wiki/Picea_mariana "Picea mariana")*: Black spruce
* *[Picea glauca](/wiki/Picea_glauca "Picea glauca")*: White spruce
* *[Pinus strobus](/wiki/Pinus_strobus "Pinus strobus")*: White pine
* *[Taxodium distichum](/wiki/Taxodium_distichum "Taxodium distichum")*: Bald cypress
* *[Tsuga canadensis](/wiki/Tsuga_canadensis "Tsuga canadensis")*: Hemlock
* *[Ulmus americana](/wiki/Ulmus_americana "Ulmus americana")*: American elm
* *[Populus tremuloides](/wiki/Populus_tremuloides "Populus tremuloides")*: Quaking aspen
* *[Populus balsamifera](/wiki/Populus_balsamifera "Populus balsamifera")*: Balsam poplar
* *[Nyssa aquatica](/wiki/Nyssa_aquatica "Nyssa aquatica")*: Water tupelo
* *[Nyssa ogeche](/wiki/Nyssa_ogeche "Nyssa ogeche")*: White tupelo
* *[Nyssa sylvatica](/wiki/Nyssa_sylvatica "Nyssa sylvatica")*: Black tupelo
* *[Alnus glutinosa](/wiki/Alnus_glutinosa "Alnus glutinosa")*: Common alder

#### Shrubs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Shrubs")]

* *[Alnus rugosa](/wiki/Alnus_rugosa "Alnus rugosa")* Tag elder
* *[Ilex verticillata](/wiki/Ilex_verticillata "Ilex verticillata")* Winterberry
* *[Ilex mucronata](/wiki/Ilex_mucronata "Ilex mucronata")* Mountain holly
* *[Sambucus racemosa](/wiki/Sambucus_racemosa "Sambucus racemosa")* Red elderberry
* *[Gaylussacia baccata](/wiki/Gaylussacia_baccata "Gaylussacia baccata")* Huckleberry
* *[Taxus canadensis](/wiki/Taxus_canadensis "Taxus canadensis")* Canadian yew
* *[Lonicera canadensis](/wiki/Lonicera_canadensis "Lonicera canadensis")* American fly honeysuckle
* *[Lonicera oblongifolia](/wiki/Lonicera_oblongifolia "Lonicera oblongifolia")* Swamp fly honeysuckle
* *[Vaccinium angustifolium](/wiki/Vaccinium_angustifolium "Vaccinium angustifolium")* Low sweet blueberry
* *[Vaccinium myrtilloides](/wiki/Vaccinium_myrtilloides "Vaccinium myrtilloides")* Canada blueberry
* *[Ribes americanum](/wiki/Ribes_americanum "Ribes americanum")* Wild black currant
* *[Ribes triste](/wiki/Ribes_triste "Ribes triste")* Swamp red currant
* *[Ribes lacustre](/wiki/Ribes_lacustre "Ribes lacustre")* Swamp black currant

#### Vines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Vines")]

* *[Toxicodendron radicans](/wiki/Toxicodendron_radicans "Toxicodendron radicans")* Poison ivy
* *[Lonicera dioica](/wiki/Lonicera_dioica "Lonicera dioica")* Limber honeysuckle

#### Ferns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Ferns")]

* *[Osmunda cinnamomea](/wiki/Osmunda_cinnamomea "Osmunda cinnamomea")* Cinnamon fern
* *[Thelypteris palustris](/wiki/Thelypteris_palustris "Thelypteris palustris")* Marsh fern
* *[Osmunda spectabilis](/wiki/Osmunda_spectabilis "Osmunda spectabilis")* Royal fern
* *[Gymnocarpium dryopteris](/wiki/Gymnocarpium_dryopteris "Gymnocarpium dryopteris")* Oak fern

#### Gramminoids

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Gramminoids")]

A variety of grasses and sedges may be present including multiple varieties of *[carex](/wiki/Carex "Carex")*.

* *[Glyceria striata](/wiki/Glyceria_striata "Glyceria striata")* Fowl manna grass

#### Mosses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Mosses")]

* *[Callicladium haldanianum](/wiki/Callicladium_haldanianum "Callicladium haldanianum")* Callicladium moss
* *[Sphagnum centrale](/wiki/Sphagnum_centrale "Sphagnum centrale")*

#### Orchids

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Orchids")]

* *[Cypripedium calceolus](/wiki/Cypripedium_calceolus "Cypripedium calceolus")* Yellow lady’s-slipper
* *[Platanthera hyperborea](/wiki/Platanthera_hyperborea "Platanthera hyperborea")* Tall northern bog orchid

#### Forbs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coniferous_swamp&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Forbs")]

* *[Aquilegia canadensis](/wiki/Aquilegia_canadensis "Aquilegia canadensis")* Red Columbine
